events.event_description: Diplomatic relations and the American Legation at Constantinople (Istanbul) established when David Porter presented credentials as Chargé d'Affaires.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations and the American Legation at Constantinople (Istanbul) were established on September 13, 1831, when David Porter presented his credentials as Charges d'Affaires.

events.event_description: The American Legation was elevated to Embassy status on June 18, 1906. Ambassador John G. A. Leishman presented credentials on October 5, 1906.

evidence.quote: The American Legation was elevated to Embassy status on June 18, 1906, when John G. A. Leishman presented his credentials as the Ambassador on October 5, 1906.

events.event_description: Turkey severed diplomatic relations with the United States on April 20, 1917, following the U.S. declaration of war against Germany.

evidence.quote: Turkey severed diplomatic relations with the United States on April 20, 1917, after the United States declared war against Germany on April 4, 1917.

events.event_description: The United States and Turkey reestablished diplomatic relations on February 17, 1927, after an exchange of notes in Angora, Turkey.

evidence.quote: The United States and Turkey reestablished relations on February 17, 1927, after an exchange of notes in Angora, Turkey.
